# Eval Task 01 — Comparison Article (frozen 2026-07-02; do not edit)

**articleType**: comparison
**Language**: English (US)
**Target**: 1,600–2,000 words
**Topic**: LiFePO4 (LFP) vs. NMC battery packs for cold-chain warehouse AGVs
(automated guided vehicles) operating at −25°C.

**Audience**: fleet operations managers and equipment engineers at 3PL cold
storage facilities in North America.

**Supplied claims (treat as context_pack.key_claims; invent nothing beyond
these):**

1. LFP cycle life: 3,000–10,000 cycles to 80% capacity (Source: Battery Power
   Tips, DiGiacomandrea, 2023).
2. LFP usable voltage window 2.5–3.65 V/cell; 3.1–3.3 V from 90% to 10% SOC
   (Source: same).
3. Charging standard lithium cells below 0°C (32°F) causes lithium plating —
   permanent capacity loss (Source: same).
4. LFP self-discharge: 2–10% per month depending on cell quality (Source: same).
5. NMC energy density typically 150–220 Wh/kg vs LFP 90–160 Wh/kg
   (Source: industry datasheet ranges; mark as Assumption if unverified).

**Constraints**: standard workflow output requirements apply (TL;DR, FAQ, CTA,
Self-Audit). No first-person war stories (no author_experience_notes supplied).
